result.finding: Two volumes configured for the oracle-db service: a named volume 'oracle_data' for Oracle database data persistence, and a bind mount './oracle-init' for initialization scripts. The application service (photoalbum-java-app) has no volume mounts — photo data is stored as BLOBs in Oracle DB, not on the filesystem.
evidence: docker-compose.yml oracle-db service: named volume 'oracle_data' -> /opt/oracle/oradata (Oracle data persistence)
docker-compose.yml oracle-db service: bind mount './oracle-init' -> /container-entrypoint-initdb.d (DB init scripts)
docker-compose.yml photoalbum-java-app service: no volumes section
values: oracle_data (named volume) -> /opt/oracle/oradata — purpose: database data persistence
No application-level volumes (photo data stored as Oracle BLOBs)
